top_categories.workout_context: 78 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
121ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Gran Fondo Guadeloupe, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Guadeloupe's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before December.
Gran Fondo Guadeloupe's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 78 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Guadeloupe. Train it until it feels boring.
9,462ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
9,462ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Base fitness for Gran Fondo Guadeloupe. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Low-cadence force work for 9,462ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 78 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.heat: December in Capesterre-Belle-Eau, Guadeloupe can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Gran Fondo Guadeloupe. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Guadeloupe’s 78 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Guadeloupe. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Guadeloupe requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 78 miles of mixed terrain in Capesterre-Belle-Eau, Guadeloupe.
